title: Episode
description: A specific installment of a show. Episodes inherit show-level fields they don't override: content_rating defaults to the show's baseline, guest_talent is additive to the show's recurring talent, and topics add context beyond the show's genre.

episode_id.description: Unique identifier for this episode within the show

show_id.description: Parent show reference. Required when the product spans multiple shows (has multiple entries in show_ids on the product).

scheduled_at.description: When the episode airs or publishes (ISO 8601)

duration_seconds.description: Expected duration of the episode in seconds

flexible_end.description: Whether the end time is approximate (live events, sports)

valid_until.description: When this episode data expires and should be re-queried. Agents should re-query before committing budget to products with tentative episodes.

content_rating.description: Episode-specific content rating. Overrides the show's baseline content_rating when present.

topics.description: Content topics for this episode. Uses the same taxonomy as the show's genre_taxonomy when present. Enables episode-level brand safety evaluation beyond content_rating.

guest_talent.description: Episode-specific guests and talent. Additive to the show's recurring talent.

ad_inventory.description: Break-based ad inventory for this episode. For non-break formats (host reads, integrations), use product placements.

derivative_of.description: When this episode is a clip, highlight, or recap derived from a full episode. The source episode_id must reference an episode within the same response.

episode_id.description: The source episode this content is derived from

type.description: What kind of derivative content this is
